purpose: Make the NIP-59 gift-wrap unwrap path (shared by kind-444 Welcomes and kind-21059 join requests) cryptographically authenticated: verify the kind-13 seal's schnorr signature, bind rumor.pubkey to the authenticated seal.pubkey, and sign the join-request seal on send so the enforcement doesn't drop every request. Two coupled halves shipped as one story per architecture.md's explicit 'MUST NOT split' rule.

public_api.name: unwrapGiftWrap
public_api.signature: async function unwrapGiftWrap(giftWrapEvent: { pubkey: string; content: string }, signer: EventSigner): Promise<AuthenticatedUnwrapResult>
public_api.description: BREAKING return-shape change (pre-authored seam contract, stories.json top-level `seams`): previously resolved to the flattened inner rumor object; now resolves to { pubkey, authenticated, rumor }. `pubkey` is always the seal's pubkey (never the raw, self-claimed rumor.pubkey). `authenticated` is true iff the seal's schnorr signature verifies (nostr-tools/pure verifyEvent) AND rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey. Never throws for a message that decrypts successfully but fails authentication -- only decryption/parse failures (a gift wrap not meant for this handler) still throw, unchanged from before.

public_api.name: AuthenticatedUnwrapResult
public_api.signature: interface AuthenticatedUnwrapResult { pubkey: string; authenticated: boolean; rumor: { id: string; pubkey: string; created_at: number; kind: number; tags: string[][]; content: string; sig: string } }
public_api.description: The seam type S4 consumes directly for auto-accept correlation (stories.json seams[0].contract). S4 MUST correlate on `pubkey` gated by `authenticated`, never on the raw rumor.pubkey, and MUST NOT redefine what 'authenticated' means.

public_api.name: buildGiftWrap
public_api.signature: async function buildGiftWrap(rumor: Omit<JoinRequestRumor, 'id'>, requesterPrivateKeyHex: string, adminPubkeyHex: string): Promise<{ id, pubkey, created_at, kind, tags, content, sig }>
public_api.description: BREAKING param change: replaces the abstract `signer: EventSigner` parameter with a raw `requesterPrivateKeyHex` so the function can call nostr-tools/nip59's `wrapEvent` directly (the same audited helper directMessages.ts's sealAndWrap uses) instead of hand-rolling an unsigned kind-13 seal. wrapEvent signs the seal with a genuine schnorr signature and sets seal.pubkey/rumor.pubkey from the real signing key, closing AC-AUTH-0. Confined to this module's sole call site (JoinRequestCard.tsx), which already held the raw privateKeyHex in scope -- no EventSigner abstraction was lost in practice (join requests never went through a NIP-46 signer).

public_api.name: sendJoinRequest
public_api.signature: async function sendJoinRequest(params: { requesterPubkeyHex, adminPubkeyHex, nonce, groupName, requesterPrivateKeyHex: string, requesterName?: string }): Promise<void>
public_api.description: Same param change as buildGiftWrap (signer -> requesterPrivateKeyHex), otherwise unchanged: builds the rumor, signs+wraps it, and raw-WebSocket-publishes to DEFAULT_RELAYS exactly as before.

dependencies_allowed: joinRequestHandler.ts (handleJoinRequest, JOIN_REQUEST_KIND) -- unchanged existing dependency, now called with the authenticated pubkey instead of the raw rumor pubkey
dependencies_forbidden: app/src/lib/directMessages.ts -- architecture.md declares it a read-only PATTERN donor (consult unwrapAndOpen's steps as the reference, do not import from it), so no new import edge was added from either touched file onto directMessages.ts

contract.consumer_artifact: app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts auto-accept correlation check (S4, not yet written)

fields.name: pubkey
fields.semantics: Always the seal's pubkey. Never the raw, self-claimed rumor.pubkey, regardless of the `authenticated` value.

fields.name: authenticated
fields.semantics: True iff the seal's schnorr signature verifies (nostr-tools/pure verifyEvent) AND rumor.pubkey === seal.pubkey. A value of false MUST NOT satisfy any trust decision, including S4's AC-AUTO-4 correlation match.

fields.name: rumor
fields.type: { id: string; pubkey: string; created_at: number; kind: number; tags: string[][]; content: string; sig: string }
fields.semantics: The unsigned inner rumor as decrypted. rumor.pubkey is the rumor's own self-claimed field (used internally to compute `authenticated`, and equal to `pubkey` when authenticated is true) -- callers must use the top-level `pubkey` field for any trust decision, never `rumor.pubkey` directly.
